definitions.definition: Motion in a plane is the motion of an object in a two-dimensional space, where the object can move in any direction on a flat surface.
Displacement is the shortest distance between the initial and final positions of an object in a specific direction, and it is a vector quantity.

definitions.example: The position vector of an object at a point (x, y) is given by the vector r = x i + y j.
definitions.definition: A position vector is a vector that points from the origin to the position of an object in a two-dimensional space.

definitions.example: A car moving on a road with a speed of 50 km/h relative to the road is an example of relative motion.
definitions.definition: Relative motion is the motion of an object with respect to a reference frame or another moving object.

definitions.example: A car moving in a circular track with a constant speed is an example of uniform circular motion.
definitions.definition: Uniform circular motion is the motion of an object in a circular path with a constant speed.

definitions.example: The centripetal acceleration of a car moving in a circular track is given by a = v^2 / r.
definitions.definition: Centripetal acceleration is the acceleration of an object moving in a circular path, directed towards the center of the circle.

definitions.example: The centripetal force of a car moving in a circular track is given by F = (m * v^2) / r.
definitions.definition: Centripetal force is the force required to keep an object moving in a circular path, directed towards the center of the circle.

definitions.example: A football kicked by a player is an example of projectile motion.
definitions.definition: Projectile motion is the motion of an object under the influence of gravity, launched from a certain height and angle.

definitions.example: The range of a projectile is given by R = (u^2 * sin(2θ)) / g.
definitions.definition: Range is the maximum horizontal distance traveled by a projectile, measured from the point of projection.

definitions.example: The time of flight of a projectile is given by T = (2 * u * sin(θ)) / g.
definitions.definition: Time of flight is the total time taken by a projectile to reach the ground, measured from the point of projection.

definitions.example: The maximum height of a projectile is given by H = (u^2 * sin^2(θ)) / (2 * g).
definitions.definition: Maximum height is the maximum vertical distance reached by a projectile, measured from the point of projection.

definitions.example: The trajectory of a projectile is a parabola.
definitions.definition: Trajectory is the path followed by a projectile in its motion under the influence of gravity.

definitions.example: The x-component of a vector is given by A_x = A * cos(θ).
definitions.definition: A component of a vector is a scalar quantity representing the magnitude of the vector in a specific direction.

definitions.example: The magnitude of a vector is given by |A| = sqrt(A_x^2 + A_y^2).
definitions.definition: Magnitude of a vector is a scalar quantity representing the length of the vector.

definitions.example: Two vectors A and B can be added by using the parallelogram law of vector addition.
definitions.definition: Vector addition is the process of combining two or more vectors to produce a resultant vector.

definitions.example: Two vectors A and B can be added using the parallelogram law by placing the head of A at the tail of B.
definitions.definition: Parallelogram law of vector addition is a graphical method of adding two vectors by drawing them head-to-tail in a parallelogram.

definitions.example: A vector can be resolved into its x and y components using the resolution formulas.
definitions.definition: Resolution of a vector is the process of breaking down a vector into its components in a specific direction.
